content.problemStatement:
Initially, there is a magical stone of mass 2N2^N2N lying at the origin of the number line. For the next NNN seconds, the following event happens:


Let us define the decomposition of a magical stone as follows: If there is a magical stone of mass M>1M > 1M>1 lying at coordinate XXX, then it decomposes into two magical stones, each of mass M2\frac{M}{2}2Mâ€‹ lying at the coordinates Xâˆ’1X-1Xâˆ’1 and X+1X+1X+1 respectively. The original stone of mass MMM gets destroyed in the process.
Each second, all the magical stones undergo decomposition simultaneously.
Note that there can be more than one stone at any coordinate XXX.

Given a range [L,R][L, R][L,R], find out the number of stones present at each of the coordinates in the range [L,R][L, R][L,R]. As the number of stones can be very large, output them modulo (109+7)(10^9 + 7)(109+7).


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains three integers NNN, LLL and RRR, as described in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each testcase, output in a single line a total of (Râˆ’L+1)(R-L+1)(Râˆ’L+1) space-separated integers. The ithi^{th}ith integer will denote the number of stones present at X=(L+iâˆ’1)X = (L+i-1)X=(L+iâˆ’1) coordinate.
As the number of stones can be very large, output them modulo (109+7)(10^9 + 7)(109+7).
constraints: Sum of (Râˆ’L+1)(R-L+1)(Râˆ’L+1) over all the test cases doesn't exceed 10510^5105.
